_comment: Attack scenarios reference points by ROLE, not by a hard-coded point name. An environment maps each role to a real point and supplies the point model (config) and the power-flow grid. The console lets an attack run on either environment, so one scenario plays out on the simple 4-bus lab and on the realistic regional grid (grid-demo) without duplicating the scenario. To add a role, give it an entry in every environment.

simple.label: Simple lab (4-bus)
simple.description: The original four-bus teaching grid. Small and legible: one tie, two feeders, a transformer. Best for seeing an attack's mechanics in one screen.

realistic.label: Realistic regional grid (grid-demo)
realistic.description: The moderate regional grid behind grid-demo: two generation plants, a 345 kV backbone, four 138 kV load substations, two external ties, ~110 points. Opening the Central-East tie triggers a real multi-substation cascade. Best for showing an attack the way an operator would actually see it.
